title: HTTP API Pod start is not the same as the CLI command
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Using the HTTP API, starting a Pod will result in an error:
```shell
ERRO[0000] error joining network namespace for container 199a9bf03917b51743986e0795cee428480b5096f316c5503fcdabd6a3c4d010: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8: unknown FS magic on "/run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8": 1021994 
Error: error joining network namespace of container 199a9bf03917b51743986e0795cee428480b5096f316c5503fcdabd6a3c4d010: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8: unknown FS magic on "/run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8": 1021994
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a pod:
```shell
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{ "name": "test01" }' --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/pods/create'
{"Id":"e95c85426946a0ed67e4dd166842172015980f76be00384fa61b76c9363794de"}
```

2. Start the pod:
```shell
curl -X POST --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/pods/test01/start'
{"Errs":null,"Id":"e95c85426946a0ed67e4dd166842172015980f76be00384fa61b76c9363794de"}
```
3. ```podman ps```:
```shell
ERRO[0000] error joining network namespace for container 199a9bf03917b51743986e0795cee428480b5096f316c5503fcdabd6a3c4d010: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8: unknown FS magic on "/run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8": 1021994 
Error: error joining network namespace of container 199a9bf03917b51743986e0795cee428480b5096f316c5503fcdabd6a3c4d010: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8: unknown FS magic on "/run/user/1000/netns/cni-a7f2b5ff-cab2-75c5-08b6-331686794bf8": 1021994
```

**Describe the results you received:**
See above.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Create pod:
```shell
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{ "name": "test01" }' --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/pods/create'
```
Start pod and ```podman ps``` without errors:
```shell
podman pod start test01
7aa1f443b9d5709a04f4f4d1c7efb70eefb982acd126b0fc4e5a3adbe908fccc

podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                 COMMAND  CREATED         STATUS             PORTS   NAMES
9d3bf01a8056  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5           22 seconds ago  Up 20 seconds ago          7aa1f443b9d5-infra
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Git Commit:   51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39
Built:        Wed Apr 21 23:34:03 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    - container_id: 1

  kernel: 5.12.3-arch1-1

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `pacman -Q info podman`):**

```
Name            : podman
Version         : 3.1.2-1
Description     : Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods
Architecture    : x86_64
URL             : https://github.com/containers/libpod
Licenses        : Apache

                  catatonit: --init flag support
                  crun: support for unified cgroupsv2
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical

comments.body: rootless, every command as regular user.
Can't reproduce locally. Can you give further details as to your setup? Is Podman managed by systemd in this case?
I am not sure what is asked, There are podman.service and podman.socket which are both active. I don't however know if it affects anything.
after some reinstalls i do not have this issue any longer
